novel_features.name: GTD next actions
novel_features.description: Your GTD 'what should I do now?' list — incomplete Next-Action tasks, sorted by priority then due date, optionally scoped to a context or goal.
novel_features.rationale: Reads the local SQLite mirror and joins tasks to contexts/goals offline, so it never re-fetches the whole task set or burns the 100-call token budget.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs the single best next action, this answers offline in one call instead of refetching and re-filtering every task.

novel_features.name: Weekly review buckets
novel_features.description: The full GTD weekly review in one pass: inbox (untriaged), overdue, stalled projects, waiting-for, and someday/maybe.
novel_features.rationale: Five cross-entity aggregations computed over the local store at once — no single Toodledo endpoint returns this, and the live MCP version refetches everything each time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Hands an agent the entire weekly-review state in one offline payload so it can drive the Sunday-night ritual without rate-limit risk.

novel_features.name: Stalled projects
novel_features.description: Folders (projects) that have open tasks but zero Next Actions — the GTD failure mode that silently stalls progress.
novel_features.rationale: A folders-to-tasks anti-join over the local store; the API has no endpoint that expresses 'projects missing a next action'.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surfaces the highest-value weekly-review bucket on its own so an agent can prompt the user to define a next action.

novel_features.name: Goal progress rollup
novel_features.description: Per-goal counts of completed vs incomplete contributing tasks, rolled up the lifetime/long-term/short-term goal hierarchy.
novel_features.rationale: Joins tasks to goals and walks the 'contributes' self-reference to roll child goals into parents — a recursive cross-entity query only the local store makes possible.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets an agent report whether a user's goals are actually being advanced by their day-to-day tasks.

novel_features.name: GTD dashboard
novel_features.description: A one-screen status board: incomplete task counts by status, priority, folder, and context, plus overdue, due-today, and starred totals.
novel_features.rationale: Computes every axis in a single offline pass over the local store; the generic analytics command groups by only one field at a time and costs an API call per run.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Gives an agent the whole task-system state at a glance without N separate grouped queries.

novel_features.name: Sync cost preview
novel_features.description: Forecasts how many of your 100 per-token API calls an incremental sync would spend, before fetching any rows.
novel_features.rationale: Calls only account/get.php for the per-resource edit cursors, diffs them against the local cursors, and reports the projected call count against Toodledo's hard rate limit.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Toodledo locks you out after 100 calls per token; this tells an agent whether a sync is safe to run before spending the budget.

novel_features.name: Batch capture
novel_features.description: Add many tasks from a file or stdin (one title per line), resolving folder/context names to ids, in budget-aware batches of 50.
novel_features.rationale: Uses Toodledo's batch tasks=<JSON> write param to add up to 50 tasks per call instead of one call per task, staying under the rate limit on big inbox loads.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Turns a pile of captured ideas into Toodledo tasks in a handful of calls rather than dozens, without exhausting the token budget.

narrative.headline: Every Toodledo feature from the terminal, plus an offline SQLite mirror, GTD reviews, and rate-budget-aware sync that no other Toodledo tool has.
narrative.value_prop: Toodledo caps you at 100 API calls per access token, so a naive wrapper is unusable. toodledo-pp-cli mirrors your whole task universe into local SQLite, then runs GTD next-actions, weekly review, stalled-project detection, goal rollups, and full-text search entirely offline — with JSON on every command and a complete agent-native MCP surface. sync-cost tells you what a refresh will spend before you spend it.
narrative.auth_narrative: Toodledo uses OAuth 2.0. Register an app at toodledo.com to get a client id and secret, set TOODLEDO_CLIENT_ID and TOODLEDO_CLIENT_SECRET, then run 'toodledo-pp-cli auth login' to authorize in your browser. Access tokens last two hours and are refreshed automatically; the refresh token expires after 30 idle days, after which you re-run auth login. The token endpoint sits behind Cloudflare and occasionally returns 403 to valid requests — the CLI treats that distinctly from a real 401 auth failure.

quickstart.comment: Check the binary, config, and Toodledo API reachability before anything else
Authorize via OAuth in the browser; tokens are stored and auto-refreshed
Mirror your whole Toodledo account into local SQLite once
Your GTD next actions for @work, answered offline from the mirror
The full weekly review (inbox, overdue, stalled, waiting, someday) with zero API calls

troubleshoots.symptom: 403 Forbidden during auth login or sync
troubleshoots.fix: Toodledo sits behind Cloudflare, which intermittently WAF-blocks valid API traffic. Wait a minute and retry; the CLI sends a real User-Agent and reports 403 distinctly from a 401 auth failure.

troubleshoots.symptom: 401 / token_rejected after a couple of hours
troubleshoots.fix: Access tokens expire after 2 hours and refresh automatically. If the refresh token expired (30 days idle), run 'toodledo-pp-cli auth login' again.

troubleshoots.symptom: Subtasks set with --parent are silently dropped
troubleshoots.fix: parent/child links require a Toodledo Pro subscription; non-Pro accounts sync the task without the link. Confirm your plan with 'toodledo-pp-cli account get'.

troubleshoots.symptom: Locked out after a burst of commands
troubleshoots.fix: Toodledo allows 100 calls per access token. Run 'toodledo-pp-cli sync-cost' to preview a sync's cost and prefer 'sync --since 7d' over '--full' for routine refreshes.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when an agent or script needs to read or mutate a Toodledo GTD system offline and cheaply: listing next actions, running a weekly review, capturing tasks in bulk, rolling up goal progress, or querying task data with SQL. The local SQLite mirror means most reads cost zero API calls, which matters because Toodledo caps you at 100 calls per access token. It is also the right tool when you want JSON-shaped task data an agent can act on directly.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for other task managers (Todoist, TickTick, Asana, Things) — it speaks only the Toodledo v3 API.
Do not use it to mass-rewrite or mass-delete a user's task history without explicit confirmation; writes hit the live Toodledo account.
Do not use it for real-time collaboration, sharing, or calendar features the Toodledo API does not expose.

recipes.title: Morning next actions
recipes.explanation: The @work next-action list as agent-shaped JSON, answered offline from the local mirror.

recipes.title: Weekly review, narrowed for an agent
recipes.explanation: Pull only the overdue titles/dates and stalled-project names from the five-bucket review so the agent does not ingest the full payload.

recipes.title: Budget-safe sync
recipes.explanation: Preview how many of your 100 token-calls a 7-day incremental sync will spend before running it.

recipes.title: Bulk capture from a file
recipes.explanation: Add one task per line, resolving the Inbox folder name, in batches of 50.

recipes.title: Find stalled projects
recipes.explanation: Projects with open tasks but no Next Action — the weekly review's highest-value bucket.
